title: A cell cannot be authored without the regen obligations its own file list implies
action:
Per D1/D2 of regen-obligation-derived. Backlog P75. Make `cells add` and `cells update` REFUSE a cell whose own `files` imply a regen obligation its `verify` does not carry.

THE RULE. If any path in the cell's `files` falls under a root the release manifest hashes, the cell's `verify` must contain `release_manifest.mjs --check` AND its `files` must contain the manifest path. If any path additionally falls under a root the ledger covers, the verify must also contain `ledger_parity.mjs --check`. Refuse otherwise, with a typed error naming the offending path, which root it hit, and the exact command to add.

DERIVE THE ROOTS, NEVER HARD-CODE THEM (D2, and this is the point of the cell). Measured today, `scripts/release_manifest.mjs:130-136` enumerates SIX roots — `.bee/bin/lib/*.mjs` (RUNTIME_LIB_DIR), `skills/**`, `.claude-plugin/skills/**` and `.codex-plugin/skills/**` (PLUGIN_SKILL_RENDER_ROOTS), `hooks/**`, and three NAMED_PLUGIN_MANIFESTS — while `scripts/ledger_parity.mjs:139-140` covers only `managed.lib` and `managed.helpers`. Do NOT copy that list into the guard. Import or otherwise derive it from the scripts themselves, so a seventh root added later is picked up without touching this guard. A hard-coded list inherits exactly the failure this cell exists to end: D20 said `templates/lib/` only, D24 corrected it to two roots, and the routing brief for THIS feature then asserted two roots again — three successive scopes, each copied from the previous summary rather than re-read from the script.

THE ESCAPE HATCH (D1). Authoring must stay unblocked, so the refusal is satisfiable by a deliberate, recorded acknowledgement on the cell — a named field the author sets explicitly, which the refusal's own message tells them about. Skipping becomes an act with a name in the cell trace, never an oversight. Choose the field name to match this repo's existing cell-schema conventions.

WHAT NOT TO BREAK. The existing `manifestLintWarning` (advisory, H2) keeps its current behavior for what it already covers — the new rule is additive and refuses; H2 is superseded only in the sense that the DERIVED obligation refuses, not that the old lint changes. Measured: both existing fixtures (`test_bee_cli.mjs:2812` uses `files: ['some/other/file.mjs']`, `:2822` uses the manifest path) touch none of the six roots, so the new rule does not fire on them and both assertions must stay green untouched.

SHOW IT BITES. Follow this repo's selftest idiom (`scripts/release_manifest.mjs:239-292`, `scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs:113-129`): exhibit the refusal firing on a cell that touches a manifest-hashed root with no `release_manifest --check`, on one that touches a ledger-covered root with no `ledger_parity --check`, and exhibit it staying silent on a cell that satisfies the obligation and on one that sets the escape hatch. A guard never seen refusing has not been shown to work — which is the entire reason P75 exists.

THIS CELL MUST PASS ITS OWN RULE. It touches `skills/**` and `templates/lib/`, so it owes both checks in its own verify and the manifest path in its own files (both are already declared above). Run the regen chain in this exact order before verifying: `node scripts/render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs`, then `node skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs --repo-root . --apply`, then `node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --write`. `onboard --apply` does not render the plugin trees; a manifest written before the render freezes stale trees as authoritative with nothing going red. Self-application is also the cheapest end-to-end proof the rule works.

truths: The trigger roots are derived from scripts/release_manifest.mjs and scripts/ledger_parity.mjs at runtime — no root path list is hard-coded in the guard, and adding a seventh root to the manifest script requires no edit here.
A cell touching a manifest-hashed root without `release_manifest.mjs --check` in its verify is REFUSED, with a typed error naming the offending path, the root it hit, and the command to add.
A cell touching a ledger-covered root without `ledger_parity.mjs --check` is refused on that ground specifically — the manifest check never stands in for it.
The escape hatch is a named, explicitly-set field recorded on the cell, and the refusal message tells the author it exists.
Both existing manifestLintWarning assertions (test_bee_cli.mjs:2812 and :2822) stay green with their fixtures untouched.
The selftest exhibits the refusal firing on both grounds AND staying silent on a satisfied cell and on an acknowledged one — recorded output, not asserted.
This cell's own verify carries both --check commands and passes them, having run the regen chain in the stated order.
artifacts: The derived-obligation guard wired into the cells add/update validation path, its escape-hatch field, new selftest rows in test_bee_cli.mjs, refreshed .bee/bin mirrors, and a regenerated release manifest and onboarding ledger
prohibitions: Never hard-code the root list — derive it from the scripts, or the guard reproduces the exact defect it exists to end.
Never weaken the refusal to a warning to get the suite green; advisory is the shape that already failed twice.
Never edit the two existing manifestLintWarning fixtures — if they red, the new rule fired where it should not have.
Never edit .bee/bin/ as the source; it is a mirror of skills/bee-hive/templates/.

verify: node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_cells.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check && node scripts/ledger_parity.mjs --check

trace.outcome: cells add/update now REFUSE a cell whose own files imply a regen obligation its verify does not carry; the trigger roots are parsed out of scripts/release_manifest.mjs and scripts/ledger_parity.mjs at every write (12 manifest roots + 2 ledger roots measured, never a list kept in the guard), with a recorded regen_obligation_ack escape hatch.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "tests_inspected": "skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs manifestLintWarning rows (:2812, :2822 — untouched, both still green), judgeStandardWarning rows, scripts/release_manifest.mjs --selftest idiom (:239-292), scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs bite-proof block (:113-129), skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/cells.mjs validateNewCell/updateCell/UPDATE_FIELD_VALIDATORS",
  "tests_added": "12 new rows in test_bee_cli.mjs covering: derivation from a synthetic guard script naming roots bee has never heard of (widgets/gizmos/.fixture-runtime); refusal on the manifest ground (missing --check) and on its second half (missing manifest path in files); refusal on the LEDGER ground specifically with the manifest check already satisfied; silence on a satisfied cell, an unrelated cell and an acknowledged cell; blind-guard throw when a present script yields no root; no-scripts host repo owes nothing; addCell end-to-end refusal writing nothing then accepting the fixed cell; updateCell end-to-end refusal on the merged shape leaving the cell untouched then accepting the fix and the ack; a boolean ack refused (the hatch must carry a reason); and a measured row against THIS checkout's real scripts printing the derived roots verbatim.",
  "red_failure_evidence": "MUTATION RED (the one that counts) — the guard was mutated into the exact defect it exists to end: deriveManifestScope made to `return { roots: ['skills','hooks'], requiredFiles: [...] }` (the 'two roots' the routing brief asserted by copying D24's summary) instead of parsing the script. Result: 255 passed, 7 failed. Verbatim: `FAIL  deriveRegenGuards reads the roots out of the guard scripts ... / the synthetic seventh root must be derived, got [\"skills\",\"hooks\"]`, `REFUSAL(manifest ground): null`, and `FAIL  measured against THIS checkout's real guard scripts / the real manifest roots must include \".bee/bin/lib\", got [\"skills\",\"hooks\"]`. SECOND MUTATION RED — the 'manifest check stands in for the ledger check' defect: the guard loop narrowed to `deriveRegenGuards(root).slice(0, 1)` so only the manifest guard runs. Result: 260 passed, 2 failed, verbatim `REFUSAL(ledger ground): null` / `FAIL  the refusal BITES on the LEDGER ground specifically — a satisfied manifest check never stands in for ledger_parity`. Both mutations reverted; the green run below is post-revert. BEFORE-STATE: prior to this cell there was no refusal at all — only bee.mjs's advisory manifestLintWarning (H2), which asks 'your verify mentions release_manifest but your files omit the manifest path?' and is therefore dodged entirely by a cell that never mentions release_manifest — the exact hole two compaction-hardening shape revisions walked through.",
  "verification_run": "node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_cells.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check && node scripts/ledger_parity.mjs --check -> exit 0. 262 passed, 0 failed (test_bee_cli, up from 250); 103 passed, 0 failed (test_cells); 4 PASS lines from test_lib_mirror including 'templates/lib and .bee/bin/lib are byte-identical (29 files)'; 'release_manifest --check: 499 file(s) match stored manifest'; 'ledger_parity --check: .bee/bin/** matches the .bee/onboarding.json managed-hash ledger'. Regen chain run in the mandated order before verifying: render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs (296 files) -> onboard_bee.mjs --repo-root . --apply (copy_lib .bee/bin/lib/cells.mjs, write_onboarding) -> release_manifest.mjs --write (499 files). Sibling suites that also exercise cells.mjs re-run green out of caution: test_misc 104/0, test_cli_cells 24/0, test_cli_state 56/0, test_state 31/0.",
  "measurement_note": "The roots were MEASURED, not recited. The routing brief and decision 531765fc both say SIX manifest roots; the derivation reads TWELVE from scripts/release_manifest.mjs — the six everyone recites plus .claude-plugin/marketplace.json, scripts/install.sh, scripts/install.ps1, scripts/test_verify_manifest.mjs and scripts/test_release_tuple.mjs (DISTRIBUTION_TOOLS/DISTRIBUTION_TESTS, hashed at release_manifest.mjs:141-142, outside the :130-136 window the brief quoted). That undercount is itself the seventh instance of the defect class, and it is exactly why no literal list was written into the guard."
}
